title: Forwarded env vars not escaped in /etc/systemd/system.conf.d/proxy-default-environment.conf
body:
### Issue Description

Windows wsl2/ubuntu 26.04
```
podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.7.0
API Version:   5.7.0
Go Version:    go1.25.0
Built:         Mon Feb 23 16:35:32 2026
Build Origin:  Ubuntu
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

Environment variables forwarded to containers are not escaped for systemd usage.

When using this:
`/etc/containers/containers.conf`:
```
[containers]
http_proxy=true

[network]
firewall_driver="iptables"
```

environment variables related to proxy are added, as-is, to:
```
/etc/systemd/system.conf.d/proxy-default-environment.conf
```

The content of the originals variables should be escaped to conform to systemd,
in my case there are modulos (%), for html chars escaping, which needs to be doubled (%%) to play well in systemd.

A one liner found to quick and dirty fix:
```
`cat /etc/systemd/system.conf.d/proxy-default-environment.conf | sed -e 's/%/%%/g' > /etc/systemd/system.conf.d/99_proxy-default-environment.conf`
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Host: /etc/environment: `PROXY=http://my%40email.com@mydomain.com`

### Describe the results you received
 podman logs: `/etc/systemd/system.conf.d/proxy-default-environment.conf:2: Failed to resolve specifiers in HTTP_PROXY=http://...`
